arguments_for_delay_or_challenge: The amendment contains a critical vulnerability allowing fee draining and XRP balance theft.
Validators were explicitly advised to vote No.
The feature was disabled in client software and replaced.

cited_facts.why_it_matters: Identifies a critical unsafe evidence pattern: a bug allowing unauthorized fee charging and balance draining, which triggers the REJECT rule for critical unsafe evidence.
Confirms an explicit no-vote advisory from validators and that the feature was not enabled, reinforcing the rejection criteria.
Indicates the feature was disabled and a replacement planned, classifying the original amendment as obsolete/retired.

output_json.decision_summary: The packet describes a critical vulnerability (fee drain) and an explicit no-vote advisory. According to the precedence rules, REJECT is used for explicit no-vote advisories, retired/obsolete proposals, or critical unsafe evidence. This amendment meets all three criteria.
output_json.forbidden_stronger_claim: The amendment is safe to enable.

override_questions: Is there any evidence that the vulnerability has been patched in this specific amendment ID?
validator_work_item.recommended_validator_action: Vote No to reject the amendment.
review_questions: Does the packet confirm the vulnerability is critical and unsafe?
validator_work_item.title: Reject PermissionDelegation due to critical fee-drain vulnerability
